3.happening: Count the {len(values)} array slots that describe the tree.
3.why: We need the size before building node objects.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id in O(1).
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, on-stack, done, or invalid — that drives the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a state.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order from slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.why: A null slot is a placeholder, not a node.

12.happening: Create node {i} with value {values[i]} and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

13.happening: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
13.why: Children come from the next slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

14.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
14.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s left child.
15.why: It is the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
17.why: The right child is the slot after the left.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

18.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
18.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s right child.
19.why: It is the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Register node {i} under its id for fast lookup.
22.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.happening: The root is slot 0 (or nothing if the tree is empty).
23.invariant: root identifies where the recursion begins.

24.happening: Set the lower bound below any real node value.
24.why: The root may hold any value, so start with no real lower limit.
24.invariant: low stands in for negative infinity.

25.happening: Set the upper bound above any real node value.
25.why: Start with no real upper limit either.
25.invariant: high stands in for positive infinity.

26.happening: Validate the tree from the root within the widest range.
26.why: Every node must fall inside the range it inherits.
26.invariant: answer becomes True only if all nodes obey their bounds.

27.happening: Return whether the tree is a valid BST: {answer}.
27.why: No node broke its inherited bounds.

30.happening: An absent child — nothing to check here.
30.why: An empty subtree can't violate the rule.

30.happening: A real node — check it against its bounds.
30.why: Every node must sit inside (lower, upper).
30.invariant: This node is about to be tested.

31.happening: Return True for the missing child.
31.why: An empty subtree is a valid BST.

32.happening: Mark node {node_id} active — its frame is on the call stack.
32.why: Amber nodes are the current root-to-node path being checked.

33.happening: Read this node's value to test against its bounds.
33.why: The value must lie strictly inside (lower, upper).

34.happening: Value {value} is at or below its lower bound — too small.
34.why: A BST node must be strictly greater than its inherited lower bound.
34.invariant: This breaks the range and fails.

34.why: It is greater than every left-ancestor it descends from.

35.happening: Mark node {node_id} invalid (red) — out of range.
35.invariant: A red node is the reason the tree fails.

36.why: One out-of-range node is enough to reject the tree.

37.happening: Value {value} is at or above its upper bound — too big.
37.why: A BST node must be strictly less than its inherited upper bound.
37.invariant: This breaks the range and fails.

37.why: It is smaller than every right-ancestor it descends from.
37.invariant: Upper bound satisfied — the node is in range.

38.happening: Mark node {node_id} invalid (red) — out of range.
38.invariant: A red node is the reason the tree fails.

39.why: One out-of-range node is enough to reject the tree.

40.happening: Validate the left child with upper tightened to {value}.
40.why: Everything left of this node must be smaller than it.

41.happening: Validate the right child with lower tightened to {value}.
41.why: Everything right of this node must be larger than it.

42.happening: This node holds only if both subtrees held.
42.why: A BST needs every subtree valid.

43.why: It and its subtrees obey the BST rule.

44.happening: Return this subtree's verdict to the parent.
44.why: The parent needs both children's results.

init.happening: Building the tree from the level-order array.
init.why: We need node objects and links before validating.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

recurse.happening: Descending into a subtree, carrying tightened bounds.
recurse.why: Each child inherits a narrower valid range.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the current path.

check.happening: Testing a node against its inherited bounds.
check.why: A valid BST node lies strictly inside (lower, upper).

update.happening: Recording state or combining subtree results.
update.why: State drives the colors; result combines both sides.

move.happening: Returning a verdict back up the tree.
move.why: A finished call hands its result to its parent.

return.why: Every node was checked against its bounds.
